channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: EU mutual assistance clause is unambiguous: Macron
item.description: The EU's mutual assistance clause is unambiguous, French President Emmanuel Macron said on Saturday, after the bloc's leaders asked officials to prepare a blueprint for how it would work amid doubts over Washington's commitment to NATO. President Donald Trump's criticism of NATO for failing to bac Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskiy arrives in Jordan for next leg of Gulf tour
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy arrived in Jordan ​on Sunday as part of a ‌Gulf tour aimed at shoring up defence ties with countries in the region amid ​rising tensions over the Iran ​war. Zelenskiy is seeking support from Gulf ⁠states for Ukraine's war against Russia ​as Western mili Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: France detains two more suspects over foiled Paris Bank of America attack
item.description: French authorities have arrested two more suspects over a ​foiled attack on Bank of America’s Paris offices, the country’s anti-terrorism prosecutor’s office said ‌on Sunday. “Two further individuals were taken into police custody last night as part of the investigatio Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: London Jewish community ambulances set ablaze in antisemitic attack, PM says
item.description: Four ambulances belonging ​to a Jewish community organisation in north London were set ablaze overnight in what British Prime ‌Minister Keir Starmer called "a deeply shocking antisemitic arson attack". The London Fire Brigade, which sent six fire engines and 40 firefighters to the scene, said Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Russia says Ukraine controls 15–17 per cent of Donetsk region
item.description: The Russian-installed head of ​Ukraine's Donetsk region, ‌Denis Pushilin, told Russian president Vladimir Putin ​at a ​meeting shown on state ⁠TV on Tuesday ​that Ukraine controls around ​15-17 per cent of the region. Russia claims the whole of ​the Donetsk region as part ​of Russia and says Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Greece sees deal to buy Italian-made frigates by April, minister says
item.description: Greece hopes to wrap up talks and sign a deal ​for the purchase of Bergamini class ‌frigates from Italy by April, Defence Minister Nikos Dendias said in an interview ​released on Sunday. High-ranking officials from ​Greece and Italy last year signed memorandums for ⁠naval cooperation, opening Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Slovakia PM Fico to meet EU's von der Leyen to push for restart of Druzhba oil flows
item.description: Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico said on Sunday he ‌would meet with EU chief Ursula von der Leyen on Tuesday, as Slovakia and Hungary ramp up pressure on Ukraine to restart Russian oil flows, warning Slovakia could block a major EU ​loan to Kyiv. Russian oil flows through the Druzhba pipelin Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Explosion hits US embassy in Oslo, causing minor damage, police say
item.description: The US embassy in Oslo was hit by an explosion early on Sunday, causing minor damage but no injuries, Norwegian police said. A loud blast was heard near the embassy compound in western Oslo at around 1am (0000 GMT), and smoke was seen rising from the area, eyewitnesses told local media. "We've de Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Plane carrying newly printed currency crashes, killing 15
item.description: Lockheed C-130 Hercules cargo plane carrying newly printed Bolivian banknotes crashed Friday near the capital, leaving at least 15 people dead, injuring others and damaging numerous vehicles, officials said. Defense Minister Marcelo Salinas said the aircraft skidded off the runway while landing at Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Buses, trams and trains grind to a halt across Germany at start of 2-day strike
item.description: Buses, trams and trains across Germany ground to a halt early Friday as local transport workers heeded a call by the Verdi public sector union to stage a strike on Feb 27 and 28. The union is aiming to gain leverage in negotiations that cover working conditions, specifically working hours and shif Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Cuba situation escalating, humanitarian needs must be solved: Kremlin
item.description: The Kremlin said on Thursday that the situation in Cuba was heating up and called for restraint after a deadly incident with a Florida-registered speedboat off the coast of the Caribbean island. Cuban forces killed four exiles and wounded six others who sailed into Cuban waters aboard a speedboat Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: UK to enforce travel permit requirement on foreign visitors
item.description: Starting on Wednesday, visitors to Britain from 85 countries must obtain an electronic permit in advance of their trip or they will be barred from travelling, the UK interior ministry said. The Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) scheme requires all visitors who do not need a visa to enter Brita Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Indonesia train crash toll rises to 14
item.description: The death toll from a train collision near the ​Indonesian capital Jakarta has risen to 14 ‌with another 84 injured, the train operator said on Tuesday, as rescuers worked to extract survivors still ​trapped in the wreckage. The collision between a ​commuter train and a long-distance train ⁠h Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump 'not happy' with latest Iran proposal to end the war
item.description: US President Donald Trump is unhappy with the latest Iranian proposal ​on resolving the two-month war, a US official said, dampening hopes for a resolution to the conflict that has disrupted energy supplies, ‌fuelled inflation, and killed thousands. Iran's latest proposal would set aside disc Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Ukraine may have to accept territorial loss for EU membership: Merz
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz suggested on Monday that Ukraine may have to accept that parts of its territory could remain outside Kyiv's control in ​a future peace deal with Russia, linking such concessions to ‌the country's prospects for joining the European Union. "At some point, Ukrain Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Pakistan still seeks to bridge US, Iran gaps despite talks setback
item.description: Work has not halted to bridge gaps between the United States and Iran, sources from mediator Pakistan said, despite the failure of face-to-face diplomacy after Donald Trump called off a trip by his envoys and told Iran to phone when it wants a deal. Hopes of reviving peace efforts have receded sin Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump safe after shooting at White House correspondents dinner, suspect in custody
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Empty tankers heading to US for oil, gas: Trump
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Kamala Harris says she might run for president again in 2028
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: US experts say American strikes on Iran may amount to war crimes
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Hopes dim for swift end to Iran war after Trump speech
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump interested in calling on Arab states to help pay for Iran war, White House says
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: US resumes embassy operations in Venezuela
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Lucrative bets that anticipated Trump's policy surprises warrant scrutiny, experts say
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Mexico's navy locates two missing sailboats carrying aid to Cuba
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: US to continue Iran strikes, pause applies only to energy sites, Semafor reports
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Death toll nearly doubles to 66 in Colombian military plane crash
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump threatens to put ICE agents in airports over funding impasse
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Myanmar's military-backed government imposes martial law in 60 townships
item.description: Myanmar's junta leader-turned-president Min Aung Hlaing has issued new emergency ordinances to impose military control in 60 townships, a move aimed at tightening security in regions still mired in conflict despite the transition to civilian rule. The ordinances cover 60 townships across Kachin, K Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Tsunami warning as 7.4 magnitude quake hits off Japan
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 7.4 struck off the northeastern coast of Japan on Monday afternoon, the Japan Meteorological Agency said. It warned of a tsunami as high as 3 metres (9.84 ft) in Iwate, Aomori and Hokkaido prefectures, reports Reuters. The tremor had an epicentre in t Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Strait ​of Hormuz ‘completely open’ to ships for remainder of ceasefire: Iran FM
item.description: Iran's foreign minister said ​on Friday that ‌passage for all commercial vessels through ​the Strait ​of Hormuz is declared ⁠completely open ​for the remaining period ​of ceasefire, in line with the ceasefire ​in Lebanon. The ​passage of vessels through the ‌strait ⁠will be on the coordi Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: India, Pakistan ‘held multiple back-channel talks’ after Operation Sindoor
item.description: Indian and Pakistani strategic experts, parliamentarians, and former diplomats have participated in at least four back-channel meetings since the high-intensity conflict in May 2025. The first of these engagements took place just two months after Operation Sindoor, according to the Hindustan Times Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Arakan Army vows to take ‘full control’ of Rakhine State by 2027
item.description: Arakan Army Commander-in-Chief Maj Gen Twan Mrat Naing has said they are expected to secure “final victory” in Rakhine State by 2027. He made the comments at an event marking the group’s 17th founding anniversary on Friday. Since late 2023, the Arakan Army has taken control of 1 Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Police fire tear gas as Noida workers' protest turns violent
item.description: Police lobbed tear gas shells and used "minimum force" on Monday to quell a factory workers' protest in the Indian national capital's suburb of Noida, which turned violent on its fourth day, with vehicles torched and stones pelted in parts of the satellite city. Noida is among the largest planned Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Iran sets 2 months to restore refiners
item.description: Iran expects to restore most damaged refining and distribution facilities to 70–80 per cent of their pre-attack capacity within one to two months, a senior oil official said, as authorities work to recover from a wave of strikes on energy infrastructure. Deputy Minister of Oil Mohammad Sadeq Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Saudi Arabia restores full capacity on East-West oil pipeline to 7 million bpd after attacks
item.description: Saudi Arabia has restored full oil pumping capacity through the East-West pipeline to about seven million barrels per day, it said on Sunday, days after providing an assessment of damage on its energy sector from attacks during the Iran conflict. The ministry said energy facilities and the pipelin Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Pranay Verma appointed as next Indian ambassador to Belgium, EU
item.description: Pranay Kumar Verma, the Indian high commissioner to Bangladesh, has been appointed as the country’s next ambassador to Belgium and the European Union. The Indian Ministry of External Affairs made the announcement in a press release on Friday. “He is expected to take up the assignment Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: India election: Assam turnout 59%, Kerala 50%, Puducherry 56%
item.description: Voting has begun across key Indian regions, with turnout gaining momentum in Assam while Kerala remains slightly behind in the current phase of elections. On Thursday, polling opened for high-stakes contests in Assam, Kerala and Puducherry, setting the tone for a competitive electoral round. Th Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: SJF President to attend Prestigious UN Global Governance Summit
item.description: In a significant recognition of international media leadership and South Asian representation in global governance, Raju Lama, International President of the SAARC Journalist Forum, will participate in the 25th Session of the United Nations Committee of Experts on Public Administration (CEPA) at th Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: India faces second year of sugar deficit as mills close early
item.description: India's sugar production is set to fall below consumption for a second straight year, as lower cane yields force mills to close faster than usual, trade officials told Reuters on Thursday. Lower output, coupled with rising exports, is likely to reduce domestic stockpiles and support local prices, Delivered by PolitePaul service
